title: Protocol for the article: “Applications of Virtual Reality Integrated with BIM in Modeling, Management, and Training in Civil Construction: A Systematic Review of the Literature”
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"fbol1","text":"This protocol presents the structure of a systematic literature review aimed at critically analyzing and synthesizing the current body of knowledge on the application of immersive technologies, especially virtual reality, in conjunction with building information modeling (BIM), to assist in processes for the AECO sectors. The review seeks to identify how these integrations can assist professionals in the modeling, training, and management stages.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5mvqj","text":"Although there are previous literature reviews on this subject, we point out the need for an updated systematic review that provides a structured and comprehensive synthesis of the studies. This approach allowed the extraction and organization of the reported data, presenting the technologies used, the processes adopted, the limitations encountered, and the favorable path for implementation in the sectors.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Table - PICO criteria (Population, Intervention, Comparison, Outcome) A B C 1 Criteria Description Synonyms 2 Population Architecture, Engineering, Construction, and Operations Sector AECO 3 Intervention Integration of BIM with Virtual Reality Building Information Modelin, VR 4 Comparison Comparison with tradicional methods 5 Outcome Application in modeling, project management, and worker training Modeling, Training and management
Preparing the generic string with the relevant terms of interest ("Building Information Modeling" OR BIM) AND ("virtual reality") AND ("construction" OR AECO) AND (management OR modeling OR training)

Defining the guiding questions for the review What implementation methodology was used? What are the engineering applications and limitations? What information model was used for information exchange? What programming language was used? What about data hosting? What tools were used for BIM and VR integration?

Databases with the largest collection of publications Science Direct - http://www.sciencedirect.com Web of Science - https://www.webofscience.com/wos/woscc/basic-search Scopus - https://www.scopus.com/home.uri
Database applied to engineering and construction Engineering Village - https://www.engineeringvillage.com/home.url?redir=t

Criteria for including files for evaluation: Inclusion criteria: IC1: Publications in indexed journals IC2: Timeframe of 10–15 years Exclusion criteria: EC1: Duplicates EC2 : Literature reviews / secondary articles EC3: Titles and keywords not aligned with the topic EC4: Abstract does not cover the integration of BIM and VR EC5: Language other than English EC6: Portrayed by the newspaper for reasons of reliability Quality criteria: QC1: Methodological analysis of integration approaches %s %s *This review included articles that addressed virtual reality when dealing with mixed or extended reality, but excluded articles that addressed only augmented reality.
Search string adapted to all databases %s %s
creator: GLÁUBER IF VIEIRA
